title: Templates
description: Explore generic template programming for functions and classes to write type-safe reusable code.
learningObjectives: Build template classes like generic vectors or buffers
Explain template instantiation and potential code bloat implications

sections.title: Compile-Time Generic Dispatch
sections.content: Templates allow you to write algorithms and containers without hardcoding specific types. When you declare `MyTemplate<int>` or `MyTemplate<float>`, the compiler instantiates a separate version of the code for each type at compile time. This ensures type safety and has zero runtime overhead (compared to casting void pointers), but can increase compiler time and binary executable sizes ('code bloat').

sections.title: Custom Math Template Structure
